member.name: Adam Jogee
member.synopsis: Adam Jogee is the Labour MP for Newcastle-under-Lyme , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Commonwealth: G20
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, If she will have discussions with her Cabinet Colleagues on the merits of inviting the Secretary General of the Commonwealth to be an associate member of the G20 when the Uni
writtenQuestions.answerText: Plans for the UK's G20 Presidency in 2027 will be announced in the normal way in due course, including any decisions on guest attendance.

writtenQuestions.heading: Equality: Staffordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what fiscal steps she is taking to reduce inequality in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and b) Staffordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving living standards for everyone and ensuring that growth is felt across all parts of the UK, including Newcastle-under-Lyme and Staffordshire. The Government’s central mission is to deliver sustained economic growth,...

writtenQuestions.heading: International Maritime Organization
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what recent discussions she has had with the International Maritime Organisation on the work of her Department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK is an active and influential member of the International Maritime Organization (IMO), where we seek to maintain the safety, security and sustainability of international maritime. The UK is an elected member of the IMO Council and is proud to host t...

writtenQuestions.heading: High Streets Organised Crime Unit: Staffordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment her Department has made of the effectiveness of the High Streets Organised Crime Unit on tackling crime in (a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and (b) Staffordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Tackling high street criminality is a priority for this Government. It is for that reason that on 19 May the Government announced a £30 million crackdown targeting cash intensive business such as barber shops, vape stores, mini-marts and sweet shops opera...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: Commonwealth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, how many visits by ministers in her department have been made to Commonwealth member states in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Details of ministerial overseas visits are routinely published in each department's quarterly transparency releases.

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Business and Trade: Commonwealth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, how many visits by ministers in his Department have been made to Commonwealth member states in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Details of the meetings held by Ministers of the Department for Business and Trade and its predecessor the Department for International trade are available on transparency pages of gov.uk and are released as part of the Government’s transparency agenda.

writtenQuestions.heading: Hair and Barber Council
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent discussions she has had with the Hair and Barber Council.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Business and Trade engages closely with hair, barbering and beauty businesses through the Personal Care Sector Roundtable, which is chaired by myself. The CEO and Registrar of the Hair and Barber Council is a longstanding member of thes...

writtenQuestions.heading: Treasury: Commonwealth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how many visits by ministers in her Department have been made to Commonwealth member states in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Information on overseas travel undertaken by HM Treasury Ministers is published regularly as part of the Department's transparency disclosures. These records are available via the GOV.UK collection "HM Treasury ministers' meetings, hospitality, gifts...

writtenQuestions.heading: Commonwealth Games 2026
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to encourage the maximum ministerial representation from across Commonwealth member states at the Commonwealth Games in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Foreign Commonwealth & Development Office (FCDO) is working closely with the Glasgow 2026 organisers, the Commonwealth Secretariat, Commonwealth Sport, and the Commonwealth network to encourage strong ministerial participation at the Glasgow 2026 ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to keep children in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and b) Staffordshire safe online.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I am grateful to my Hon. Friend the Member for Newcastle-under-Lyme for his strong advocacy on behalf of his constituents. Keeping children safe online is a priority for this government. We published the ‘Growing up in the Online World’ consultation, whic...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Investment: Staffordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to support locally led innovation investment in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and b) Staffordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring every part of the UK benefits from innovation-led growth. UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) is supporting c. £36 million of active projects in the combined areas of Shropshire and Staffordshire, helping to drive loc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Sports: Staffordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what recent steps her Department has taken to help ensure that the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games support grassroots sports in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and b) Staffordshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is proud to support the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games which is aiming to both build on the legacy of the Glasgow 2014 Games and also leave its own, including inspiring participation in grassroots sport. I was delighted to recently visit t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Young People: Staffordshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to increase the number of places that young people in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and b) Staffordshire can go to outside of school.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through Youth Matters, this Government is delivering more and better places for young people. We are investing in 250 new or refurbished youth centres alongside 50 Young Futures Hubs. We are streamlining local funding by merging our major youth funds, whi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Disorder: Belfast
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, what steps he plans to take with the Northern Ireland Executive to help tackle the potential impact of the a) attempted murder in North Belfast and b) the subsequent violence and disorder on the young pe
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is vital that we work together with the Northern Ireland Executive to ensure the safety and wellbeing of all our communities across the United Kingdom. I meet regularly with Northern Ireland Executive Ministers to discuss a range of priority issues, in...

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Visual Impairment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what discussions she has had with Cabinet colleagues on ensuring children and young people with a visual impairment in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme and b) Staffordshire get the support that they need.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Education and Department for Health and Social Care ministers are jointly engaging stakeholders to ensure our reforms work in practice. As part of this engagement, I took part in a roundtable earlier this year to discuss visual impairme...

writtenQuestions.heading: Israeli Settlements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking to support the Gulf Cooperation Council in the context of increased settler violence in the West Bank.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We continue to have regular discussions with the Gulf Cooperation Council and its member states on a range of issues including the situation in Palestine. We are committed to working together to support the rights of the Palestinian people and the viabili...

writtenQuestions.heading: Genocide
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the hon. Member for Battersea, representing the Church Commissioners, what steps the Church is taking to help support victims of genocide.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Church of England and the wider Anglican Communion looks to support all victims of armed conflict, regardless of whether war crimes or crimes against humanity have been committed, through humanitarian support, pastoral care and advocacy work.Support i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Waste: Crime
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of a single national reporting system for incidents of waste crime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Comprehensive guidance on how to report waste crime is available on GOV.UK here. The guidance provides clear signposts towards different reporting routes, based on the nature of the crime and which authority is responsible for enforcement. A single, natio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Prostate Cancer: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve early diagnosis of prostate cancer in a) Newcastle-under-Lyme, b) Staffordshire and c) England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has committed to meeting the cancer waiting times standards in England by the end of this Parliament, by modernising the whole cancer pathway so that patients are diagnosed and treated more quickly.Through the National Cancer Plan for Engla...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Rural Payments Agency
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent discussions she has had with Cabinet colleagues on the (a) effectiveness and (b) performance of the Rural Payments Agency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs regularly meets with Cabinet colleagues to address farming and food security, underlining the Government’s commitment to supporting British agriculture; ensuring the Rural Payments Agency (RPA...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

registeredInterests.category: 3. Gifts, benefits and hospitality from UK sources

items.text: Name of donor: Anton’ de Piro Address of donor: private Amount of donation or nature and value if donation in kind: Return economy flight between Northern Ireland and Great Britain, value £696 Date received: 20 June 2025 Date accepted: 20 June 2025 Donor status: individual (Registered 18 July 2025)

Name of donor: Consulate of the Republic of San Marino to the United Kingdom Address of donor: c/o Baird House 15-17 St Cross Street Farringdon London EC1N 8UW Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Airfare/Ground Transfers (£552.07) Accommodation (£322) Subsistence (£287.24), value £1,161.31 Destination of visit: San Marino and Vatican City Dates of visit: 21 October 2025 to 24 October 2025 Purpose of visit: Part of a cross-party delegation to further relations between the United Kingdom and the Republic of San Marino and the Holy See. The visit included being received at the Vatican by the Holy Father, His Holiness Pope Leo. (Registered 16 November 2025)

Name of donor: Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, Switzerland Address of donor: Embassy of Switzerland in the United Kingdom, 16-18 Montagu Place, London W1H 2BQ Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Travel (£380), accommodation (£990), in-country travel (£290) and meals (£240), value £1,900 Destination of visit: Switzerland (Berne and Zurich) Dates of visit: 5 November 2025 to 8 November 2025 Purpose of visit: Cross party delegation of MPs focused on what lessons the United Kingdom can learn from Switzerland’s rail/transport network and Vocational and their Professional Education and Training (VPET) system. (Registered 1 December 2025)
Director and Trustee of the Commonwealth Parliamentary Association (UK Branch). Date interest arose: 16 October 2024 (Registered 12 November 2024)
Member of the British Group Inter-Parliamentary Union Executive Committee. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 29 January 2026 (Registered 24 February 2026)
